pattern: Because a valid block can only be a run of zeroes immediately followed by a run of ones, you never need to inspect every substring -- just walk the run-lengths once and score each zero-run/one-run boundary by twice the smaller run.
hints: A qualifying substring can only ever look like a block of zeroes followed directly by a block of ones -- any other shape breaks the 'zeroes before ones' rule.
Scan the string once, measuring the length of each maximal run of zeroes and the maximal run of ones that comes right after it.
For each such zero-run/one-run pair, the longest balanced piece you can take from it has length twice the smaller of the two run lengths -- track the best across the whole string.

edge_inputs.description: single zero-run followed by a longer one-run
one-run longer than the zero-run before it
no zeroes at all before the ones, only the empty substring balances
ones appear before zeroes, so despite equal counts nothing balances
several zero/one run pairs, the best pair is not the first one encountered
all zeroes, no ones to pair with
all ones, no zeroes to pair with

stress.description: random binary string at the maximum allowed length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
